stories.title: Grades and takeaways from Hurricanes' dominating G...
stories.summary: The Hurricanes routed the Canadiens in Montreal. Here's what it all means, with a look ahead to Friday's Game 5.

stories.title: Andersen's shutout puts Canes 1 win from Final
stories.summary: Frederik Andersen made 18 saves for his third shutout in 12 postseason games this year, and the Hurricanes, now 11-1 in the postseason, routed the Canadiens in Game 4 to move within a game of the Stanley Cup Final.

stories.title: Ohtani dissatisfied despite L.A.'s no-hit bid, HR
stories.summary: Shohei Ohtani lamented the flaws in his performance Wednesday night -- even after hitting a leadoff home run and positioning the Dodgers for a combined no-hitter.

stories.title: Sankey: SEC best 'by far' despite B1G dominance
stories.summary: SEC commissioner Greg Sankey said his league still "stands alone" despite the Big Ten winning the past three national titles and holding a 4-0 record against the SEC over the past three seasons in head-to-head CFP matchups.

stories.title: Smart hints SEC could leave NCAA if no CFP deal
stories.summary: Conversations around conference expansion have dominated the college football spring meetings thus far, and Kirby Smart's comments on Tuesday about the SEC essentially seceding if an agreement can't be reached have created plenty of questions.
